Comment: /* */ Fixed incorrect grammar at header of article.

/* Prevailing wind */This information is not accurate (or is no longer accurate), the average cairo temperature is much colder and moderate than described, there is no citation as to what this is measuring. It's worth noting that the winter of 2025-2026 will likely dip below 7.

Of the initial four cities listed, none of them have ever had a temperature reading of 54 degrees Celsius. In fact, nowhere in Egypt has such a high reading ever been recorded. As for averages, the highest of them was Aswan which has an average daily high of 41.9° C in two months of the year as can be seen on its linked Wikipedia article. As for Luxor, its hottest month has an average daily high of 41.2° C. The two cities I deleted fail to even reach a daily average high of 40° in any month.
